subject: english

sections.heading: Definition
sections.content: Tenses occur only in verbs. Tense refers to the changes that take place in the form of the verb to indicate time. Only two tenses, namely the present and the past, can be identified.

sections.heading: Present Tense
sections.content:
The only change that takes place in the form of the verb to indicate the present tense occurs when the verb agrees with a third person singular subject.
Examples:
Singular: I sleep, wake and eat; You sleep, wake and eat; He sleeps, wakes and eats.
Plural: We sleep, wake and eat; You sleep, wake and eat; They sleep, wake and eat.

sections.heading: Past Tense
sections.content:
Regular verbs form their past by adding ‘ed’ to the simple form of the verb, while irregular verbs form their past tense in different ways.
Examples:
- Ume worked in the farm yesterday. (regular)
- Ada spoke to me on the telephone. (irregular)

Evaluation: Exercise 1, Question 1, page 205, Countdown to English.

learning_objectives: Explain Definition in Review of Tenses
Identify key ideas in Review of Tenses
Apply Review of Tenses concepts in examples
